title: KAPP-Sen TMC: Dissociation of Pancreatic Islets (non-recovered)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ddlh7","text":"The dispersed samples were shipped cold from PRODOLABS. Prior to scRNA-seq dispersed samples from brain dead donor’s pancreatic islets were dissociated as follows.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":45,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Before beginning cell dissociation coat all the materials (pipettes, tubes, etc.) with PIM-S001GMP media to prevent sticking.
Cells were fixated prior to scRNAseq according to https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.x54v9py5zg3e/v1
Transfer cell suspension of pure islets to a new 50ml tube. Use additional media to rinse original container.
Centrifuge at room temperature 130g for 5 mins.
Aspirate the supernatant and add media to the appropriate concentration of 1,000 islets/1 ml. Ex: If receiving 7,000 islets, use 7 ml media.
Add half of the islets (recovery) to the coated flask and put to incubate at 37°C.
To the other half of the islets (no recovery), add media to 10 ml and centrifuge then aspirate carefully.
Add accutase at a concentration of 1 ml/1,000 islets. Mix with pipette and incubate at 37°C. Coat pipette tip and use to mix every 2 mins, checking at 6 mins.
Add CMRL 1066 (Cat. 11530037) to approximately 9 ml media/1 ml accutase then centrifuge at 230 g.
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend in 1.5 ml of CMRL
Count cells using AO/PI (acridine orage/propidium iodide) Cell Viability Kit for Luna-FL automated cell counter.
creator: Ashley M Raynock
